title: For the One Who Hurt You
scripture_text: Jesus said to him, "I do not say to you seven times, but seventy times seven."
reflection: Forgiveness is not a feeling. It is a practice. It is choosing, again and again, to not let this person rent space in your head.
body:
You have every right to be angry. The thing that was done to you was wrong. The anger is not a sin. It is a signal that something precious was violated.

But the anger is also eating you. You have noticed that. The hurt has become a hobby. The resentment a routine. The person who hurt you is in your head more than they ever were when they were actually in your life.

Forgiveness is not saying it was okay. Forgiveness is saying: I am no longer going to carry this for you. It is heavy. It is yours. Take it back.

You do not have to feel it to do it. The feeling will come later, or it will not. The decision is what matters.
prayer: Lord, I am not ready to forgive. But I am ready to want to forgive. Start there. Amen.
